scope: Continuation proof for the public LifeMax production URL after the clean repo push and Vercel preview deploy.
risk_class: Q3 observation only; no production mutation in this continuation pass.

preview_smoke.meaning: The preview deployment reached READY in Vercel CLI, but it is not usable as public browser proof without signed-in Vercel access.

performed_actions: opened /profile and reset browser-local demo state
clicked mobile shell navigation through Capture, Patterns, Experiments, and Profile
edited and saved a day plan
marked one plan item done and one skipped
saved an evening close with tomorrow hint
started and ended a local Experiment as inconclusive
refreshed Today and verified saved state persisted visibly

final_today_evidence.heading: Check in, then keep the day honest.
final_today_evidence.saved_plan_text: Ship one real LifeMax proof loop
final_today_evidence.saved_tomorrow_hint: Smoke the public route before polishing
final_today_evidence.truth_boundary_visible: No WHOOP, account, hosted AI, or backend write is claimed.

route_smoke.heading: Check in, then keep the day honest.
Save the signal before it disappears.
Only show patterns the local data can support.
Turn a weak signal into a cautious local test.
Local app truth and data controls.
Privacy policy and local data boundary.

console_errors.note: One captured console error was sourced to vercel.com login after opening the protected preview, not to the LifeMax production URL.

truth_boundaries: This proves visible public web/demo behavior only.
WHOOP, accounts, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, database persistence, and backend writes are not connected.
The LifeMax lab repo boot/preflight remains red, so daily shared-context usability is not claimed.
No credentials, auth settings, DNS, domain aliases, production promotion, backend services, or external sends were changed in this continuation pass.
next_actions: If another deployment is required, decide whether to promote the clean repo deployment or adjust preview protection.
If Oliver wants real high-level functionality beyond local demo, start the backend/shared-context/Wave 1 goal with green local MCP and explicit integration gates.
If keeping public proof in Git matters, unignore or selectively attach screenshots; currently screenshots are local ignored artifacts by design.
